encounter.reason: Food allergy follow-up, repeat IgE monitoring
encounter.location: UNC Medical Center, 101 Manning Drive, Chapel Hill, NC 27514

labs.name: peanut-specific IgE (serum)
labs.interpretation: Class 5, very high, slightly increased from prior 48.2 kU/L

labs.interpretation: Class 4, high, slight decrease from prior 22.7 kU/L

labs.interpretation: Class 3, moderate-high, slight decrease from prior 15.1 kU/L

labs.interpretation: elevated for age, up from prior 384 IU/mL

conditions.name: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis
tree nut allergy (cashew, walnut, almond) — IgE-mediated sensitization

medications.name: fluticasone propionate inhaler 44 mcg, 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer
medications.sig: 1 puff inhaled twice daily via spacer

medications.name: albuterol inhaler 90 mcg, 2 puffs inhaled as needed via spacer
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled as needed for wheezing or shortness of breath, via spacer

medications.name: triamcinolone 0.1% ointment
medications.sig: applied to eczema flares twice daily as needed

medications.name: epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen Jr) 0.15 mg intramuscular
medications.sig: 0.15 mg intramuscular as needed for anaphylaxis, two-pack carried at all times
medications.reason: peanut allergy with history of anaphylaxis

document_references.description: Pediatric allergy/immunology subspecialty follow-up note for annual food allergy and asthma reassessment. Documents interval history, vitals, physical exam, repeat serum specific IgE panel results, five-problem assessment addressing peanut allergy, tree nut allergy, persistent asthma, atopic dermatitis, and allergic rhinitis, with plan for continued management and 6–8 month follow-up.
Updated serum specific IgE panel results including peanut, cashew, walnut, almond specific IgE values and total IgE, with comparison to prior November 2022 values.
Refreshed anaphylaxis emergency action plan for peanut and tree nut allergy. No content changes from prior version; date refreshed to 2023-10-02.
